title: [ESI][Runtime][Cosim] Switch Verilator backend to call verilator_bin directly
body:
The `esiaccel.cosim.Verilator` class called the `verilator` Perl wrapper script. Perl is poorly supported on Windows, so this switches to calling `verilator_bin` directly and handling the C++ build ourselves via CMake + Ninja.

### Changes

- **`verilator_bin` invocation**: Default binary changed from `verilator` to `verilator_bin`. Removed `--exe`, `--build`, `-CFLAGS`, `-LDFLAGS`, and `driver.cpp` from the verilator command since the build is now handled separately.
- **Backwards compatibility**: If `VERILATOR_PATH` env var points to the Perl wrapper (`verilator`), it is automatically redirected to `verilator_bin` in the same directory.
- **`_find_verilator_root()`**: Auto-discovers `VERILATOR_ROOT` from the environment variable or derives it from `verilator_bin`'s location (`$VERILATOR_ROOT/bin/verilator_bin`). Previously the Perl wrapper handled this.
- **`compile_commands()` returns full build flow**: Returns three commands run sequentially: (1) `verilator_bin` for C++ generation, (2) `cmake -G Ninja -S/-B` for configuration, (3) `ninja -C` for building. The `compile()` override is minimal — sets `VERILATOR_ROOT`, writes `CMakeLists.txt`, then delegates to `super().compile()`.
- **`_write_cmake()`**: Generates a `CMakeLists.txt` that compiles verilator-generated C++, verilator runtime sources, `driver.cpp`, and DPI shared objects.
- **Executable location**: Moved from `obj_dir/V<top>` to `obj_dir/cmake_build/V<top>`.
- **Unit tests**: 15 tests covering command generation, backwards-compatible VERILATOR_PATH handling, root discovery, CMake template output, and run command paths. All tests use pytest's `tmp_path` fixture for cross-platform safety.

### Generated CMakeLists.txt (example)

```cmake
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)
project(VESI_Cosim_Top CXX)

file(GLOB GENERATED_SOURCES "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/../*.cpp")

add_executable(VESI_Cosim_Top
  ${GENERATED_SOURCES}
  /usr/share/verilator/include/verilated.cpp
  /usr/share/verilator/include/verilated_threads.cpp
  /path/to/esiaccel/cosim/driver.cpp
)
# ... includes, definitions, thread + DPI linking
```

`VERILATOR_PATH` env var continues to work for custom binary locations, with automatic redirection from `verilator` to `verilator_bin`.

<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[ESI][Runtime][Cosim] Switch to calling `verilator_bin` directly</issue_title>
> <issue_description>Currently, `esiaccel.cosim.Verilator` calls `verilator` which is a Perl script. Perl is not well supported on Windows so for Windows support it'd probably be better to call `verilator_bin` directly. I've attached a copy.
> 
> We really only need a small portion of the support. Evaluate the support we do need and port it into `esiaccel.cosim.Verilator`. The exception is the CPP build support we're using. I think we should probably do that ourselves. I suspect it's best to write out a cmake file, run cmake on it (with the Ninja generator), then run ninja to produce the simulation executable.
> 
> [verilator.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25725672/verilator.txt)
> 
> Start by getting into an environment which can run ESI cosim. The easiest way is to use the Docker image located here: ghcr.io/circt/images/pycde-esi-test. Run the ESI runtime pytests to test. Review the esi runtime skills file to learn more about how to build and test the ESI runtime.</issue_description>
> 
> <agent_instructions>There is no reason to compile all of CIRCT so you can do all your work in the ESI runtime directory with its cmake file.</agent_instructions>
>
